mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers the discount rate applied to equities and eases financial conditions, so successive monthly declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if the sign of the relationship flips during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a high-unemployment threshold indicates weakening household income and demand, which raises recession odds and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 9.5% do not show below-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 60% across triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in RSAFS together with rising PAYEMS signals broad nominal sales growth supported by payroll income, which should lift near-term equity returns by improving revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-PAYEMS signal does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if positive 20-day returns fail to dominate when both series are accelerating together.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in INDPRO after a prior decline signals industrial activity recovery and inventory restocking, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebound episodes are not followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign is negative in more than half of rebound cases.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts breakout above its recent range marks improving credit demand and household risk appetite, which should translate into positive S&P 500 performance over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout signals are followed by non-positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if no positive return premium exists versus the unconditional sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated and FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates stay deeply negative and valuations expand, so equities should deliver positive returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction between high inflation and near-zero policy rates does not improve predictive accuracy relative to separate CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS effects, or if the signal loses significance after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in PAYEMS accompanied by rising UNRATE indicates a genuine labor-market shock that should depress S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors price weaker earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises within the same monthly release window
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if either labor series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand deceleration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as revenue growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the relationship is not materially stronger than each series used separately.
